title: quercus pubescens subsp. subpyrenaica
intro: Quercus pubescens subsp. subpyrenaica, also known as the Pyrenean downy oak, is a subspecies of oak tree native to the Pyrenees Mountains between France and Spain. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the bark of the Pyrenean downy oak is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used to support digestive health and boost the immune system. The plant's medicinal properties and health benefits make it a sought-after ingredient in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
